test.command: source .env && export PROXY_AUTH_TOKEN && bash -c 'NVIDIA_BASE="$(python3 -c "import sys,os;print(os.path.join(sys.prefix,\"lib\",f\"python{sys.version_info.major}.{sys.version_info.minor}\",\"site-packages\",\"nvidia\"))" 2>/dev/null||true)" && if [ -d "${NVIDIA_BASE:-}" ]; then for d in "$NVIDIA_BASE"/*/lib; do [ -d "$d" ] && LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$d:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:-}"; done; export LD_LIBRARY_PATH; fi && clj -M:test'
test.note: 418 tests, 3129 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors. Without PROXY_AUTH_TOKEN, 4 MT tests error (known env issue, not a code bug).

typecheck.note: Clojure is dynamically typed. No typecheck validator per AGENTS.md.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented curated corpus data pattern (data/curated/<family>/prompts.jsonl schema), cross-source dedup policy (curated preferred), canonicalize! return shape change (:dedup-report key), and analysis-functions-vs-metrics distinction in architecture.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce the explicit-requires convention with a note that it applies to test files as well as source files. Multiple workers across milestones have consistently violated 'Always use explicit :require with aliases in ns forms' in test code. Consider adding: 'This applies to ALL Clojure files including test files — never rely on transitive namespace loading even in tests.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: curated-taxonomy: test uses clojure.string without require (lines 162-163). end-to-end-validation: test uses clojure.set/intersection without require. Same pattern found in prior milestones (pipeline-spine, misc-pipeline-fixes). At least 4 separate workers have made this mistake.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Consider adding guidance on test fixture management: 'For integration/e2e tests requiring taxonomy definitions, prefer a shared test-fixture namespace over duplicating taxonomy setup in each test file.' Multiple test files (e2e_test, curated_pipeline_integration_test, curated_taxonomy_test) independently re-register the same taxonomy families/categories. If taxonomy shapes change, all test files need synchronized updates.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: end-to-end-validation: e2e_test.clj lines 62-157 redefine full taxonomy from scratch. curated_pipeline_integration_test.clj has similar extensive taxonomy setup. Same pattern across multiple test files.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The clojure-dsl-worker skill should note: (1) :params-spec is required when registering transforms — workers discover this by reading transform/registry.clj source code. (2) Mock MT pattern (with-redefs on translate-via-proxy) is the standard approach for e2e/integration tests to avoid proxy dependency — see transform_pipeline_test.clj for the established pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: curated-pipeline-integration: worker skill feedback about params-spec discovery. end-to-end-validation: worker skill feedback about mock MT pattern.

rejectedObservations.observation: random-uuid in test helpers violates determinism convention
rejectedObservations.reason: The AGENTS.md determinism convention ('All randomness must be seeded') is scoped to pipeline-producing code, not test scaffolding. Using random-uuid for non-critical test fixture fields (source-id, canonical-hash in coverage tests) is acceptable when those fields don't affect the computation under test. Codifying this as a convention change would add unnecessary constraint.

rejectedObservations.observation: Unregistered analysis function pattern needs convention documentation
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in the library update (architecture.md). The distinction between registered metrics and analysis utilities is clear from the code and now from the docs. No AGENTS.md change needed.
